member.name: T:GitCredMan.Core.Interfaces.ICredentialStore
member.summary: Read/write tokens in the OS secure store.

member.name: T:GitCredMan.Core.Interfaces.ISettingsRepository
member.summary: Persist and load application settings (no secrets).

member.name: T:GitCredMan.Core.Interfaces.IRepositoryScanner
member.summary: Scan the file system for Git repositories.

member.name: T:GitCredMan.Core.Interfaces.IGitConfigService
member.summary: Apply an Account's identity to a Repository via git-config.

member.name: T:GitCredMan.Core.Models.Account
member.summary: A stored Git identity. Tokens are NEVER stored here — they live exclusively in Windows Credential Manager.

member.name: P:GitCredMan.Core.Models.Account.HasStoredToken
member.summary: Not persisted — checked live from Credential Manager.

member.name: T:GitCredMan.Core.Models.DiscoveredIdentity
member.summary: A git identity (name + email + host) discovered by reading .git/config files across all known repositories, plus the global ~/.gitconfig.

member.name: P:GitCredMan.Core.Models.DiscoveredIdentity.Key
member.summary: Unique key for deduplication.

member.name: P:GitCredMan.Core.Models.DiscoveredIdentity.AlreadyImported
member.summary: True if this identity already exists in the account list.

member.name: T:GitCredMan.Core.Services.AccountService
member.summary: Domain service: manages the Account collection and coordinates with the credential store. This is the single source of truth for account business logic — ViewModels call this, not raw storage.

member.name: M:GitCredMan.Core.Services.AccountService.GetToken(System.String)
member.summary: Load the stored token for an account (may return null).

member.name: T:GitCredMan.Core.Services.GitConfigService
member.summary: Applies an Account's identity to a repository via git-config. Also reads the current system git identity (global user.name / user.email).

member.name: M:GitCredMan.Core.Services.GitConfigService.ReadSystemIdentityAsync
member.summary: Reads the global git identity configured on this machine via "git config --global user.name / user.email". Returns null fields if not configured.

member.name: T:GitCredMan.Core.Services.SystemGitIdentity
member.summary: The current global git identity read from the system.

member.name: M:GitCredMan.Core.Services.SystemGitIdentity.#ctor(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: The current global git identity read from the system.

member.name: T:GitCredMan.Core.Services.GitIdentityScanner
member.summary: Scans all known repositories (and global git config) for existing git identities (user.name / user.email) and returns them deduplicated. This lets users discover and import pre-existing credentials they've already configured on this machine.

member.name: M:GitCredMan.Core.Services.GitIdentityScanner.ScanAsync(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{GitCredMan.Core.Models.Repository},System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{GitCredMan.Core.Models.Account},System.IProgress{System.String})
member.summary: Scan all repositories plus the global git config. Returns deduplicated identities, each marked if already in accounts.

member.name: M:GitCredMan.Core.Services.JsonSettingsRepository.#ctor(Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.ILogger{GitCredMan.Core.Services.JsonSettingsRepository},System.String)
member.summary: Test-only constructor — pass a custom directory.

member.name: T:GitCredMan.Core.Services.RepositoryScannerService
member.summary: Recursively scans all fixed drives for .git directories. Reports progress via IProgress and supports cancellation.

member.name: T:GitCredMan.Core.Services.WindowsCredentialStore
member.summary:
Stores and retrieves tokens via Windows Credential Manager (advapi32). Blobs are encrypted at rest by DPAPI, scoped to the current user.
Credential target key: "GitCredMan:{accountId}"
